news.title: Toronto home sales fall to four-month low in October 2025
news.para: In October, the Greater Toronto Area experienced a significant drop in home sales, touching a four-month low. The primary reason for this decline appears to be buyer reluctance fueled by an unpredictable economic landscape. Although the home price index registered a slight monthly increase, it remains lower than last year.

news.title: Bajaj Housing Finance's net profit up 17.84% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Bajaj Housing Finance announced a significant 17.84 percent rise in its net  profit for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. The company's profit after tax reached ₹642.96 crore. Total income also saw a healthy increase of 14.30 percent. Assets under management grew by 24 percent, demonstrating strong business expansion.

news.title: Sweden's Skanska operating profit rises to $148.7 million in Q3 2025
news.para: Skanska, the prominent Swedish builder, faced a challenging quarter as its third-quarter earnings fell below analyst predictions. The company's operating profits took a hit from significant property write-downs and subdued demand in the residential development arena, predominantly in Sweden. However, in an optimistic note, Skanska emphasized that the broader construction market outlook remains relatively steady.

news.title: JK Lakshmi Cement posts profit of ₹80.63 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: JK Lakshmi Cement has achieved a profit of ₹80.63 crore in the September quarter, a strong recovery from last year's loss. The company's total income also saw a substantial increase. Significant expansion projects are underway, including new grinding units and increased clinker capacity.

news.title: Ajmera Realty & Infra India net profit dips 14.09% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ajmera Realty & Infra India reported a 14.09% decline in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, reaching ₹30.37 crore. Despite this, total income grew 8.30% to ₹221.01 crore, with sales value surging 48% and collections up 52%. The company plans significant launches in H2 FY26 and FY27, including office space and uber-luxury residential projects.

news.title: Singapore's CapitaLand Investment exceeds target with $650 million for lodging fund
news.para: CapitaLand Investment has wrapped up its latest lodging fund, reaching an impressive $650 million—$50 million beyond its initial goal. The CLARA II fund is set to enhance CapitaLand's assets under management by a striking $1.6 billion.

news.title: Godrej Properties' net profit grows 20.73% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Godrej Properties announced a significant 20.73 percent rise in its net profit for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. The company's profit after tax reached ₹402.99 crore. Total income also saw a substantial increase of 44.82 percent. Booking value grew by 64 percent year-on-year. Godrej Properties is on track to exceed its annual booking value guidance for FY26.

news.title: Case against real estate developer for ₹100 crore housing fraud in Mumbai
news.para: Authorities have taken decisive action, launching a case against a real estate developer and his partners over allegations of swindling 102 individuals out of Rs 100 crore. Reports indicate that money meant for the Sky 31 housing project in Wadala was misused for personal expenditures.

news.title: About 170 vacant site owners in Trichy get notices for poor upkeep
news.para: Trichy Corporation is actively identifying and issuing notices to owners of poorly maintained vacant plots, warning them to clear overgrown weeds and level the ground to prevent water stagnation.  So far, 170 notices have been served, with owners given a grace period to comply or face penalties and charges for cleaning.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA set to seek objections before acquiring land for Indirapuram extension
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority will now seek public objections for land acquisition for its Indirapuram Extension scheme following an Allahabad High Court order. The court directed GDA to pay up to twice the 2014 circle rate to 35 farmers for 153 acres of unacquired land, after a prolonged legal battle spanning over 18 years.

news.title: Stamp registration services to pause in Noida & Ghaziabad between Nov 8-11 for server migration
news.para: Property registrations in Noida and Ghaziabad will be suspended from November 8 to 11 due to a server migration to the National Govt Cloud. This temporary halt aims to enhance system efficiency and data security, though it is expected to cause a significant revenue dip for the state exchequer.

news.title: Chennai development body to construct co-working spaces in George Town
news.para: Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority (CMDA) is constructing a three-storey Chief Minister's co-working space on Wall Tax Road, George Town, to support the growing office workforce from lower-income neighborhoods. The facility will offer 150 work desks, internet-connected computers, and competitive exam reading material, aiming for operational status by December.

news.title: Hyderabad: HMDA to auction 44 acres in Kokapet, Moosapet, aims ₹5,000 crore revenue
news.para: Following TSIIC's record land auctions, HMDA is now e-auctioning 44 acres in Kokapet and Moosapet, aiming for Rs 5,000 crore. Upset prices range from Rs 70 crore to Rs 99 crore per acre, with Kokapet's Neopolis and Golden Mile layouts seeing significant interest due to prime location and connectivity.

news.title: Land acquisition for New Noida project to begin soon
news.para: Land acquisition for the upcoming industrial township, New Noida (DNGIR), is commencing with a hybrid model involving direct purchase, mutual consent, and land pooling. Noida Authority is finalizing compensation plans for farmers across 80 villages in Gautam Budh Nagar and Bulandshahr districts, with rates influenced by proximity to the airport and existing land values.

news.title: Delhi: Signature View residents to get rent from July 2023
news.para: Delhi Development Authority (DDA) will pay facilitation fees to Signature View Apartments residents displaced due to structural damage. The monthly compensation, starting at Rs 50,000 for HIG and Rs 38,000 for MIG flats, will increase by 10% annually until 2026. This decision follows a Delhi High Court order and aims to alleviate the financial burden on affected families.

news.title: Odisha REAT upholds homebuyers' rights in landmark ruling
news.para: The Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal (OREAT) upheld a homebuyer's flat allotment, deeming its cancellation by a promoter arbitrary and illegal. The tribunal ruled the promoter violated the agreement and RERA Act by demanding balance payment before sale deed execution. OREAT ordered the promoter to accept the balance, execute the deed, and hand over possession, reinforcing buyer rights.

news.title: The Ramco Cements' net profit jumps three-fold to ₹78 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ramco Cements has made headlines this quarter with an impressive profit increase, tripling to Rs 77.88 crore for September. The company also saw a 9.52 percent rise in revenue from operations, maintaining stable cement sales volumes. With a forward-looking approach, Ramco aims to boost its cement production capacity to 30 MTPA by March 2026, driven by innovative projects and enhancements.

news.title: Mindspace REIT's NOI grows 26% to ₹634 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Mindspace Business Parks REIT has struck a triumphant note this quarter, showcasing a 26% rise in net operating income. Further sweetening the news, unitholders are set to benefit from a 16.3% boost in distributions.

news.title: Officials reclaim prime land in Malavalli town following Karnataka HC order
news.para: Mandya officials reclaimed 2.3 acres of prime land in Malavalli town, valued at over Rs 50 crore, following a high court order. The property, previously held by the family of former Union minister MV Chandrashekara Murthy, was officially handed over to the city municipal council. The land will now be utilized for public benefit.

news.title: Indore: Parts of 140 houses demolished in Malviya Nagar to pave way for master plan roads
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation demolished sections of 140 homes in Malviya Nagar. This action clears the path for a new 60-foot wide road. The road will connect MR-9 to LIG square, serving as a vital link. Residents received prior notice and some voluntarily removed parts of their houses. This development is part of the city's Master Plan to manage future traffic.
